mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during periods of declining INDPRO, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation combined with elevated policy rates forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary conditions, which increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of cases when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.01, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that rapidly erodes consumer confidence and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with simultaneous declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production alongside rising housing starts signals strengthening economic output and construction demand, which improves equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year backtest.
